headline: MSTR is pinned below a massive 190 GEX call wall in an extreme call-skew regime while TSLA momentum has lifted spot to the dominant 430 dealer-gamma ceiling; both stocks are in long-gamma environments favoring credit-spread sellers this week.

setup.gamma_note: Net GEX +58B; dealers are long gamma so they sell into rallies and buy dips, compressing realized vol and anchoring spot near the 187.5-190 cluster.

bull.path: BTC accelerates above 82,000 and drags MSTR through the 190 call wall; gamma flip triggers short-covering toward the 200 secondary GEX supply cluster.
triggers: MSTR closes above 190 on volume 2x the 10-day average
NVRP drops below 0.95 as calls aggressively reprice higher

base.path: Long gamma pins MSTR in the 183-192 range; extreme call skew (7th percentile) slowly fades as spot oscillates near the 187.5-190 dealer cluster through the May 15 expiry.
triggers: Front P/C spread stays below -4.0 without widening sharply

bear.path: BTC softness below 79,000 drags MSTR through 183; breach of the 187.5 put wall flips local dealer gamma negative and accelerates a move toward 180, then 175 structural support.
triggers: MSTR closes below 183 with expanding put OI at 180-175 strikes
NVRP rises above 1.20 as vol bid tilts put-side

trades.rationale: Fade extreme call skew (7th percentile) at the dominant 190 GEX call wall where $6.5B in dealer long-gamma creates a mechanical ceiling; long-gamma regime suppresses upside breakouts, making 190 a high-probability short-term cap.
trades.exit: Buy back for $1.00 (69% of max profit captured), or stop if MSTR closes above 190 on volume at least 2x the 10-day average.

trades.rationale: Sell put premium into the 180 GEX put wall (-$800M dealer gamma) where mechanical hedging flows provide structural support; collect elevated front ATM IV (69%) with a clearly defined downside.
trades.exit: Buy back for $0.90 (68% of max profit captured), or stop if MSTR closes below 181 with BTC below 79,000.

trades.rationale: Secondary upside hedge at the 195-200 GEX supply cluster; the extra DTE allows additional theta decay while BTC beta risk (2.37, correlation 0.85) remains capped below 82,000 where MSTR is trading at a +3.34pt BTC-implied premium.
trades.exit: Buy back for $0.80 (68% of max profit), or stop if spot closes above 195 on two consecutive sessions.

risks: A BTC gap move above 82,000 could override dealer gamma pinning and trigger a call-wall breach at 190, invalidating both call credit spreads.
MSTR is trading at a +3.34pt premium to its BTC-implied fair value; mean-reversion compression could be fast and steep.
Skew percentile sample size is only 14 sessions, making the 7th-percentile reading statistically unreliable.
RV30 at 69% means a 5%+ daily move can rapidly erode the favorable risk-reward on short credit positions.
exits: Close all credit spreads if MSTR prints a daily close above 192 (clear break of 190 GEX wall) or below 179 (below secondary put wall).
Reduce net short-vega exposure by 50% if BTC drops more than 5% intraday.
Roll short strikes one expiry forward if NVRP falls below 0.90, signaling a shift to buyer-edge IV regime.

setup.gamma_note: Net GEX +134.5B; the dominant 430 call wall alone holds $43.5B in dealer long-gamma, creating an unusually strong magnetic ceiling; expect strong mean-reversion forces near 428-430.

bull.path: A volume surge above 430 triggers dealer short-covering and a gamma flip; spot accelerates toward the 435-440-450 secondary GEX cluster on broad macro risk-on sentiment.
triggers: TSLA closes above 432 on volume 40% above its 20-day average
Macro catalyst such as a surprise dovish Fed statement or strong macro data print
430 call OI rolls up to 440 overnight indicating institutional upside repositioning

base.path: Spot gravitates to the 430 GEX magnet through May 15 expiry; IV continues compressing from already-low 23rd percentile as long gamma keeps realized vol subdued in the 420-432 range.
triggers: No major macro catalyst this week
NVRP stays 1.05-1.12, confirming mild IV overpricing persists

bear.path: Rejection at the 430 call wall sends spot back to 420 put support; a close below 420 opens a fast move toward the 410-400 put wall cluster.
triggers: Broad equity selloff with SPX down more than 1.5% on the day
430 call OI declines by more than 30% intraday signaling holders exiting

trades.rationale: Sell the dominant 430 GEX call wall where $43.5B in dealer long-gamma mechanically suppresses breakouts; NVRP at 1.08 (seller edge) confirms call premium is moderately overpriced versus realized vol.
trades.exit: Buy back for $1.50 (67% of max profit), or stop if TSLA closes above 432 on volume exceeding the 20-day average by 40% or more.

trades.rationale: Monetize dealer long-gamma support at the 420 put wall (-$2.1B GEX) and 400 put wall (-$3.0B GEX); the 415 short strike gives a 13-point buffer from spot while both GEX put walls provide layered mechanical support below.
trades.exit: Buy back for $1.00 (69% of max profit), or stop if TSLA closes below 420 on two consecutive sessions.

risks: The $43.5B 430 call wall is historically concentrated; a gamma flip above 430 could be unusually violent with limited dealer hedging supply to absorb moves above that strike.
IV at the 23rd 2-year percentile is near historical lows; any vol expansion event (earnings pre-announcement, macro shock) could reprice options sharply higher, hurting short-vega positions.
NVRP is declining (1.13 yesterday to 1.08 today); if it crosses below 1.0, the seller-edge rationale for the call spread weakens.
TSLA rallied approximately 15% in 10 sessions (372 to 428); an extended move increases mean-reversion risk that could gap past the 430 GEX ceiling if momentum is strong enough.
exits: Close all spreads immediately if TSLA prints a daily close above 433 (gamma flip zone) or below 418 (approaching 420 GEX put wall).
Take profits on the bull put spread early if TSLA retests 422-420 intraweek; buy back at $1.00 or less.
Close the call credit spread if the 430 call open interest drops by more than 25% intraday, signaling a gamma-flip event is in progress.
